steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No action" option in the "Add an action to your declarative agent" dropdown within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog of Visual Studio Code (Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension).
Click the "Default folder (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects)" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Type "Add Capability" into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" option in the VS Code command palette to initiate the Add Capability workflow.
Click the "Copilot connector" option in the "Add Capability" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Enter a Copilot connector Connection ID" option in the Copilot connector Content dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Type 'testconnector' into the "Connection ID" input field in the VS Code command palette.
Press Enter to confirm the "testconnector" input in the Connection ID prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog in Visual Studio Code to select the manifest file from the suggested path.
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "appPackage" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "declarativeAppManifest.json" file within the "appPackage" folder in the VS Code Explorer sidebar to select or open the file.
Click on the "testconnector" value within the "connection_id" field in the declarativeAgent.json file opened in Visual Studio Code.
